mandatory_Instructions_To_AI_Assistant.overview: This file must be read using a tool, understood, and acted on by an AI Assistant prior to executing every User Prompt
mandatory_Instructions_To_AI_Assistant.notes: This is a prototype for discussion only
purpose: The purpose of this file is to bridge the semantic gap between language used by different classes of user, and that of the AI Assistant
It provides pre-defined 'Curated Prompts' that are chosen by the AI Assistant according to the User Prompt and are appended to the User Prompt
There currently are two classes of target user:
1. Human users unskilled in writing AI prompts, but who can describe their requirements well enough for the Assistant to select appropriate 'Curated Prompts' from this file
2. Software clients who directly reference one or more Curated Prompts using identifiers, and may or may not provide a supplementary natural language prompt
use: The AI Assistant reads the User Prompt then consults this file to clarify the intent of the User, and from this selects one or more 'Curated Prompts'
This selection is done by using the curatedPrompts 'description' property to choose the curatedPrompts that most closely match the Users requirements
A 'Curated Prompt' is text that augments the User Prompt
A 'Curated Prompt' can be either a request or a format
A context is the problem that the AI Assistant must solve, or action that it must take to provide a response
A format is how the completion is returned, e.g., concise plain text, verbose plain text, image, structured data, etc
The final Prompt that the AI Assistant executes is the original User Prompt prepended to a compilation of the selected supplementaryPromptText values with explanatory text to glue them together

mapping.mandatory_Instruction_To_AI_Assistant: The AI Assistant must consult each 'description' property in the curatedPrompts arrays to discover the one or more items that match the User Prompt most closely.

responseFormats.name: assistantFeedback
responseFormats.purpose: Focus on facts, logical order, clarity, and conciseness.

responseFormats.name: expositoryText
responseFormats.purpose: Focus on facts, logical order, clarity, and conciseness.

responseFormats.name: descriptiveText
responseFormats.purpose: Create a picture through imagery to help the reader visualize scenes, emotions, or experiences.

responseFormats.name: tabular
responseFormats.purpose: Row ordering reflects data content, e.g., temporal, magnitude, similarity.  Columns reflect main data properties. UserPrompt provides further guidance.

responseFormats.name: spreadsheet
responseFormats.format: A spreadsheet in .csv format unless indicated otherwise in the user prompt.
responseFormats.purpose: Row ordering reflects data content, e.g., temporal, magnitude, similarity.  Columns reflect main data properties. UserPrompt provides further guidance.

responseFormats.name: ffMpegSubclipping
responseFormats.purpose: Times in hh:mm:ss format of in and out-points of subclips

responseFormats.name: curatorSubclipping
responseFormats.purpose: Timecodes in SMPTE hh:mm:ss:ff format of in-and out-points of subclips

responseFormats.name: soccerSpatioTemporal
responseFormats.purpose: Progress of ball towards opposition goal measured in average speed/number of passes. UserPrompt provides further guidance.

responseFormats.name: soccerSpatioTemporal
responseFormats.purpose: Sequences of play extracted and transformed into graph data for loading into Neo4j,
